reasoning: The baseline value is 1.000 (which corresponds to 0 events over the past 360 days) on Dec 11, 2025. This means there were exactly zero recorded events of "Violence against civilians" in Mayotte by ACLED's methodology over the past year. For the resolution criteria to be met, Mayotte must experience at least 11 events (>10 times the baseline 1.000 = >10 = 11+) within a 30-day period before each respective resolution date. ACLED defines "Violence against civilians" as acts committed by organized armed groups against unarmed non-combatants. The types of violence prominent in Mayotte (gang violence, youth riots, clashes with French police/gendarmerie) are fundamentally coded as "Riots", "Protests", or not included at all, rather than "Violence against civilians" by organized armed actors. Given that we would need a sudden, massive onset of classic armed conflict involving a recognized armed group systematically targeting civilians at a rate of roughly one event every 3 days, and with zero foundation for such a shift in the current media coverage, the probability that 11 or more such events are recorded in Mayotte in a 30-day window before any of these dates is effectively zero. I assign a minimal nonzero probability of 0.02 evenly to all dates as there has been no trajectory towards escalation of this specific ACLED event type.
